mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >60% of instances where unemployment rises >0.2pp over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive 60d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5d returns during expansion periods, with a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in >65% of months following CPI MoM spikes above 0.4%, or the signal shows no predictive power in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust job creation and economic resilience, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence fails to predict positive 60d returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with improved corporate revenue forecasts and equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20d forward returns across multiple business cycles, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and earnings downgrade risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns remain positive in >60% of weeks following industrial production drops exceeding 0.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during manufacturing downturns.
